author: Tim Rice <tim.j.rice@hackrange.com>
name: htaccess_bypass
summary: High-fidelity check of an alleged access-control bypass: distinguishes real bypass from 404 / unrestricted resource / 403 noise.

args.--cookie: Cookie header sent on EVERY request (auth + anon baselines + variants). Use this when the orchestrator's challenge route resolves a session cookie via auth.form_login_cookie() and the probe should test under that authenticated context.
args.--auth-cookie: Optional separate cookie used ONLY to compare bypass responses against an authenticated baseline (alternative to --cookie when you want to test the bypass anonymously but still know what an auth-success looks like).
args.--baseline-404: Optional URL of a guaranteed-404 path on this host, used as the negative-control fingerprint. If omitted, the probe synthesizes one by appending a random suffix to the URL's directory.
